mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations and improve corporate borrowing conditions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following rate cuts show a negative mean or Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in greater than 55% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and residential investment demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance at p less than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to generate positive forward returns during recovery periods, with a mean return difference indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month inflation reading above 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets exhibit positive forward returns in greater than 60% of high-inflation threshold breaches over a 5-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real gross domestic product confirms robust economic expansion and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of payroll and GDP growth yields a negative or flat forward return distribution with a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a housing market correction signal weakening residential investment and associated financial sector stress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Falling housing starts fail to predict negative equity returns during housing downturns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in the industrial production index reflects improving factory activity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The industrial production trend signal generates negative forward returns in greater than 55% of observations over a 5-day horizon.
